                    have := ih (idx1 := ⟨i-1, by
                      cases i with
                      | zero => simp at h1
                      | succ i' =>
                        simpa using (Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ (by
                          simpa [Nat.succ_eq_add_one] using hi))
                    ⟩) (idx2 := ⟨j-1, by
                      cases j with
                      | zero => simp at h2
                      | succ j' =>
                        simpa using (Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ (by
                          simpa [Nat.succ_eq_add_one] using hj))
                    ⟩) hEq'
                    simpa [List.eraseIdx, h1, h2] using this

intro idx1 idx2 hEq
          cases' idx1 with i1 hi1
          cases' idx2 with i2 hi2
          cases i1 using Nat.casesOn with
          | zero =>
            cases i2 using Nat.casesOn with
            | zero =>
              simpa using hPerm
            | succ j2 =>
              -- impossible: head equals a later element in (x :: l2) but RHS head is x
              have : x = l2[j2] := by
                simpa [List.getElem_cons, Nat.succ_eq_add_one] using hEq
              -- remove head on left, remove succ on right, then use IH after swapping x forward
              have : (l1.Perm (l2.eraseIdx j2)) := by
                -- from (x :: l1) ~ (x :: l2), cancel x then erase matching element
                have hxmem : l2[j2] ∈ l2 := by
                  exact List.get_mem _ _
                have hxmem' : x ∈ l2 := by simpa [this] using hxmem
                have : x ∈ (x :: l2) := by simp
                have : x ∈ (x :: l1) := by
                  exact (hPerm.subset (by simp)) this
                have : x ∈ l1 := by
                  simpa [List.mem_cons] using this
                -- not needed explicitly; rely on permutation lemma after erasing matching on right via IH on swapped perm
                exact (hPerm.trans (by
                  -- (x :: l2).eraseIdx (succ j2) = x :: (l2.eraseIdx j2)
                  -- so it suffices to show l1.Perm (l2.eraseIdx j2)
                  simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_succ] using (List.Perm.refl (x :: l2)))).tail
              -- simplify the goal directly
              simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_zero, List.eraseIdx_cons_succ] using (IH (idx1 := ⟨0, by simpa using Nat.zero_lt_succ _⟩) (idx2 := ⟨j2, by simpa using Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ hi2⟩) (by simpa [List.getElem_cons] using this))
          | succ j1 =>
            cases i2 using Nat.casesOn with
            | zero =>
              -- symmetric to previous impossible case
              have : l1[j1] = x := by
                simpa [List.getElem_cons, Nat.succ_eq_add_one] using hEq
              have : x = l1[j1] := this.symm
              simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_zero, List.eraseIdx_cons_succ] using (IH (idx1 := ⟨j1, by simpa using Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ hi1⟩) (idx2 := ⟨0, by simpa using Nat.zero_lt_succ _⟩) (by simpa [List.getElem_cons] using this))
            | succ j2 =>
              -- both indices in the tail: reduce to IH
              have hEq' : l1[j1] = l2[j2] := by
                simpa [List.getElem_cons, Nat.succ_eq_add_one] using hEq
              have := IH (idx1 := ⟨j1, by simpa using Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ hi1⟩) (idx2 := ⟨j2, by simpa using Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ hi2⟩) hEq'
              simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_succ] using this

cases h2
                  | succ j =>
                    have hj' : j < l2.length := by simpa using (Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ hv)
                    -- use IH to erase matching x's at head and at k+1, then perm to erase at j+1; simplify
                    have hx0 : (x :: l1)[⟨0, by simpa [List.length_cons]⟩] = (x :: l2)[⟨k.1 + 1, by simpa [List.length_cons] using Nat.succ_lt_succ k.2⟩] := by
                      simpa [List.getElem_cons_zero, hk']
                    have hbase := IH (idx1 := ⟨0, by simpa [List.length_cons]⟩)
                                      (idx2 := ⟨k.1 + 1, by simpa [List.length_cons] using Nat.succ_lt_succ k.2⟩) hx0
                    -- left side: eraseIdx 0 gives l1; right side: eraseIdx (k+1) gives (x::l2).eraseIdx (k+1)
                    have hbase' : l1.Perm ((x :: l2).eraseIdx (k.1 + 1)) := by
                      simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_zero] using hbase
                    -- now show ((x::l2).eraseIdx (k+1)) perm (l2.eraseIdx j)
                    have : ((x :: l2).eraseIdx (k.1 + 1)).Perm (l2.eraseIdx j) := by
                      -- eraseIdx (succ j) on (x::l2) is eraseIdx j on l2
                      have : ((x :: l2).eraseIdx (Nat.succ j)) = (l2.eraseIdx j) := by
                        simp [List.eraseIdx_cons_succ]
                      -- use list_Perm_eraseIdx to change erased index among equal elements in permuted lists (refl perm)
                      -- simplify via this equality by rewriting with idx2 = succ j and choosing same
                      simpa [this]
                    exact hbase'.trans this
              -- finally simplify eraseIdx on (x::l1) at idx1=0 and on (x::l2) at idx2=succ j
              cases idx1 with
              | mk v hv =>
                simp only [Fin.val_mk] at h1
                subst v
                cases idx2 with
                | mk v2 hv2 =>
                  simp only [Fin.val_mk] at h2
                  cases v2 with
                  | zero => cases h2
                  | succ j =>
                    simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_zero, List.eraseIdx_cons_succ] using this
          | succ i =>
            cases h2 : idx2.val with
            | zero =>
              -- symmetric to previous case
              have hx : (x :: l2)[idx2] = x := by
                cases idx2 with
                | mk v hv =>
                  simp only [Fin.val_mk] at h2
                  subst v
                  simp
              have : (x :: l1)[idx1] = x := by simpa [hx] using hEq
              cases idx1 with
              | mk v hv =>
                simp only [List.length_cons, Fin.val_mk] at h1
                cases v with
                | zero => cases h1
                | succ j =>
                  have hj : j < l1.length := by simpa using (Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ hv)
                  -- reduce to previous case by symmetry using Perm.symm
                  have h' : (l2.eraseIdx 0).Perm ((x :: l1).eraseIdx (Nat.succ j)) := by
                    have hx0 : (x :: l2)[⟨0, by simpa [List.length_cons]⟩] = (x :: l1)[⟨j + 1, by simpa [List.length_cons] using Nat.succ_lt_succ hj⟩] := by
                      simpa [List.getElem_cons_zero, this]
                    have := (perm_eraseIdx (l1 := (x :: l2)) (l2 := (x :: l1)) (hPerm := (List.Perm.cons x hPerm).symm)
                      (idx1 := ⟨0, by simpa [List.length_cons]⟩)
                      (idx2 := ⟨j + 1, by simpa [List.length_cons] using Nat.succ_lt_succ hj⟩)) hx0
                    simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_zero] using this
                  have : ((x :: l1).eraseIdx (Nat.succ j)).Perm l2 := by
                    simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_succ] using h'.symm
                  simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_succ, List.eraseIdx_cons_zero] using this
            | succ j =>
              -- both indices in tail, reduce to IH
              have hi1 : i < l1.length := by
                cases idx1 with
                | mk v hv =>
                  simp only [List.length_cons, Fin.val_mk] at h1
                  subst v
                  simpa using (Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ hv)
              have hi2 : j < l2.length := by
                cases idx2 with
                | mk v hv =>
                  simp only [List.length_cons, Fin.val_mk] at h2
                  subst v
                  simpa using (Nat.lt_of_succ_lt_succ hv)
              have hEq' : l1[⟨i, hi1⟩] = l2[⟨j, hi2⟩] := by
                -- strip heads
                cases idx1 with
                | mk v hv =>
                  cases idx2 with
                  | mk v2 hv2 =>
                    simp only [List.length_cons, Fin.val_mk] at h1 h2
                    subst v; subst v2
                    simpa [List.getElem_cons_succ] using hEq
              have hPerm' := IH (idx1 := ⟨i, hi1⟩) (idx2 := ⟨j, hi2⟩) hEq'
              -- lift back under cons
              cases idx1 with
              | mk v hv =>
                cases idx2 with
                | mk v2 hv2 =>
                  simp only [List.length_cons, Fin.val_mk] at h1 h2
                  subst v; subst v2
                  simpa [List.eraseIdx_cons_succ] using hPerm'

intro idx1 idx2 hEq
            cases idx1 using Fin.cases with
            | zero =>
                cases idx2 using Fin.cases with
                | zero =>
                    simp at hEq
                    subst hEq
                    simpa using hPerm
                | succ idx2' =>
                    have hx : x ∈ (x :: l₂) := by simp
                    have hx' : x ∈ (x :: l₂).eraseIdx (Fin.succ idx2') := by
                      simpa [List.eraseIdx] using List.mem_eraseIdx_of_mem hx (by simp)
                    have : False := by
                      simp [List.eraseIdx, List.getElem_cons_succ] at hEq
                      exact this.elim
                    contradiction
            | succ idx1' =>
                cases idx2 using Fin.cases with
                | zero =>
                    have : False := by
                      simp [List.getElem_cons_succ] at hEq
                      exact this.elim
                    contradiction
                | succ idx2' =>
                    have hEq' : l₁[idx1'] = l₂[idx2'] := by
                      simpa [List.getElem_cons_succ] using hEq
                    simpa [List.eraseIdx] using ih (idx1 := idx1') (idx2 := idx2') hEq'

subst h2
                simpa
              | inr h2 =>
                rcases h2 with ⟨j, hj⟩
                subst hj
                simp at hEq
            | inr h1 =>
              rcases h1 with ⟨i, hi⟩
              subst hi
              cases hx2 with
              | inl h2 =>
                subst h2
                simp at hEq
              | inr h2 =>
                rcases h2 with ⟨j, hj⟩
                subst hj
                have hEq' : l₁[i] = l₂[j] := by
                  simpa using hEq
                have := IH (idx1 := i) (idx2 := j) hEq'
                simpa using this

intro idx1 idx2 hEq
          cases h1 : idx1.val with
          | zero =>
            have hx1 : idx1 = ⟨0, by simpa [h1] using idx1.isLt⟩ := by
              cases idx1 with
              | mk v hv =>
                simp at h1
                subst h1
                rfl
            subst hx1
            cases h2 : idx2.val with
            | zero =>
              have hx2 : idx2 = ⟨0, by simpa [h2] using idx2.isLt⟩ := by
                cases idx2 with
                | mk v hv =>
                  simp at h2
                  subst h2
                  rfl
              subst hx2
              simpa using hPerm
            | succ j =>
              have hjlt : j < l2.length := by
                have : idx2.val < (x :: l2).length := idx2.isLt
                simpa [List.length_cons, h2, Nat.succ_lt_succ_iff] using this
              have hmem : l2[j] = x := by
                have : (x :: l1)[0] = (x :: l2)[idx2] := hEq
                simpa [List.getElem_cons, h2] using this
              have hswap : (x :: l2).eraseIdx idx2 = (l2.eraseIdx ⟨j, hjlt⟩) := by
                simp [List.eraseIdx, h2]
              have hleft : (x :: l1).eraseIdx (0 : Nat) = l1 := by rfl
              subst hleft
              rw [hswap]
              have : (x :: l2).Perm (l2.insertIdx j x) := by
                simpa [List.insertIdx, h2] using (List.perm_middle.trans (by
                  simpa [List.insertIdx, h2, List.set] ))
              have : l2.eraseIdx ⟨j, hjlt⟩ = (l2.eraseIdx ⟨j, hjlt⟩) := rfl
              simpa using (hPerm.eraseIdx ⟨j, hjlt⟩)
          | succ i =>
            have hilt : i < l1.length := by
              have : idx1.val < (x :: l1).length := idx1.isLt
              simpa [List.length_cons, h1, Nat.succ_lt_succ_iff] using this
            cases h2 : idx2.val with
            | zero =>
              have hx2 : idx2 = ⟨0, by simpa [h2] using idx2.isLt⟩ := by
                cases idx2 with
                | mk v hv =>
                  simp at h2
                  subst h2
                  rfl
              subst hx2
              have hmem : l1[i] = x := by
                have : (x :: l1)[idx1] = (x :: l2)[0] := hEq
                simpa [List.getElem_cons, h1] using this
              simp [List.eraseIdx, h1]
              exact (IH (idx1 := ⟨i, hilt⟩) (idx2 := ⟨0, by simpa using (Nat.zero_lt_succ l2.length)⟩) (by simpa [List.getElem_cons] using hmem)).symm
            | succ j =>
              have hjlt : j < l2.length := by
                have : idx2.val < (x :: l2).length := idx2.isLt
                simpa [List.length_cons, h2, Nat.succ_lt_succ_iff] using this
              have hEq' : l1[⟨i, hilt⟩] = l2[⟨j, hjlt⟩] := by
                have : (x :: l1)[idx1] = (x :: l2)[idx2] := hEq
                simpa [List.getElem_cons, h1, h2] using this
              simp [List.eraseIdx, h1, h2]
              exact IH (idx1 := ⟨i, hilt⟩) (idx2 := ⟨j, hjlt⟩) hEq'
        | swap x y l =>
          intro idx1 idx2 hEq
          cases h1 : idx1.val <;> cases h2 : idx2.val <;> simp at hEq ⊢
        | trans h1 h2 IH1 IH2 =>
          intro idx1 idx2 hEq
          exact (IH1 hEq).trans (IH2 rfl)
